query: utility of unenhanced fat-suppressed T1-weighted MRI in differentiating bone infarcts from acute osteomyelitis in children with sickle cell disease

retrieved_docs.title: Sickle Cell Anemia (Nursing) -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: A chest X-ray shall be performed in patients with respiratory signs and symptoms, but it may be normal in the early phase of acute chest syndrome. Plain radiography of the peripheries is used in identifying acute and subacute marrow infarctions, as well as observing old infarcts. In the case of osteomyelitis, early radiographs are not useful because they do not show significant changes. However, in the subsequent two weeks, the plain radiographs may reveal the destruction of the bone, periosteal bone formation, sinus tract, and sequestra. MRI scans are paramount in diagnosing osteomyelitis and avascular necrosis of the femoral and humeral heads. Technetium-99 scans are also used to detect osteonecrosis.

retrieved_docs.title: Sickle Cell Disease (Archived) -- Evaluation -- Imaging
retrieved_docs.content: Have a low threshold to obtain chest x-rays to diagnose acute chest syndrome. Get a sonogram of the abdomen as needed. Transcranial sonograms are recommended as screening tests from age two years to prevent strokes. Other imaging studies such as bone scan, MRI, and MRA can be considered depending on clinical suspicion. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Bone Scan -- Normal and Critical Findings -- Critical Findings
retrieved_docs.content: Detection of avascular necrosis: history of sickle cell disease or chronic steroid use should raise suspicion for this entity

retrieved_docs.title: Sickle Cell Disease (Archived) -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Acute anemia Hemoglobin C disease Hemolytic anemia Legg-Calve-Perthes disease Leukemias Osteomyelitis Pulmonary embolism Rheumatoid arthritis Septic arthritis

retrieved_docs.title: Sickle Cell Crisis -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Vaso-occlusive crisis presents with severe pain with a relative lack of objective clinical signs. Differential diagnosis should include conditions specific to the site of the pain and not be blindly attributed to sickle cell disease. As an example, patients presenting with an abdominal painful crisis can mimic an acute abdomen, and the differential should include conditions that give acute abdominal pain, including acute appendicitis, acute pancreatitis, acute pyelonephritis, pelvic inflammatory disease, and hepatobiliary disease. Avascular necrosis and acute osteomyelitis are a consideration when there is persistent local bone pain.

retrieved_docs.title: Sickle Cell Anemia -- Evaluation -- Acute Complications in Patients with SCA
retrieved_docs.content: The risk factors for ACS include a previous history of ACS, asthma, or recent events like recent surgical procedures, pulmonary embolism, fluid overload, infection, etc. The clinical features include sudden onset of cough and shortness of breath. Fever may or may not be a part of the spectrum of presentation. If present, then it usually points towards infection. Laboratory evaluation includes a complete blood count with differential chemistries, including liver and kidney evaluation, blood cultures, and sputum cultures. Chest X-ray shows a new pulmonary infiltrate- this is a quintessential feature of defining ACS. CT and perfusion mismatch scans are only used if there is a strong clinical suspicion of pulmonary or fat embolism. Therefore, they are not usually helpful in acute settings. Sequestration Crises: This can either be hepatic or splenic sequestration.

retrieved_docs.title: Osteomyelitis Imaging -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Osteomyelitis is a multifaceted disease characterized by inflammation of bone and marrow. While various etiologies of osteomyelitis have been documented, it is almost always secondary to infection. Osteomyelitis may occur through direct inoculation of bacteria into the bone, hematogenous spread from distant sites of infection, or the contiguous spread from nearby soft tissues, termed nonhematogenous infection. Common causative organisms of osteomyelitis include Staphylococcus aureus , Streptococcus spp., and Enterobacteriaceae . In patients with specific comorbidities, such as sickle cell disease, other etiologic infectious agents, such as Salmonella spp. and Escherichia coli , are more common. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Vertebral Osteomyelitis -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: When evaluating vertebral osteomyelitis, the following differential diagnoses should also be considered: Chronic nonbacterial osteomyelitis Ewing sarcoma Gout Gaucher disease Malignancy Pseudogout Septic arthritis Sickle cell anemia Vitamin C deficiency Vertebral compression fracture Vaso-occlusive pain episode

retrieved_docs.title: Sickle Cell Anemia -- Evaluation -- Chronic Complications in Patients with SCA
retrieved_docs.content: Avascular Necrosis (AVN) of Joints: AVN of the femoral head is a common cause of chronic pain and disability in SCA patients. Although the hip joint is the most common joint to be involved, other joints can also be affected. AVN occurs at the distal portion of the bone, where collateral circulation is poor. The capillaries get occluded by sickle RBCs, leading to hypoxia and bone death. Risk factors for AVN of the femoral head include age, frequency of painful episodes, hemoglobin level, and alpha-gene deletion. In patients with HbSS, the overall prevalence is 50 percent by age 33. HbSS-alpha thalassemia and HbSS-Beta-0 thalassemia are at higher risk of developing AVN early in life.

retrieved_docs.title: Osteomyelitis Imaging -- Magnetic Resonance
retrieved_docs.content: The earliest indication of acute osteomyelitis revealed by MRI is bone marrow edema, which can be observed as soon as 1 to 2 days following the onset of infection. Early identification of characteristic findings eliminates waiting for the traditional 10 to 14 days required to diagnose osteomyelitis using plain radiography. Bone marrow contains fat and normally results in high signal intensity on T1-weighted MR images. In acute osteomyelitis, the bone marrow becomes congested with fluid and pus, producing low T1-weighted signal intensity and high signal intensity on fluid-sensitive and post-contrast sequences such as short tau inversion recovery (STIR). [8] Imaging of the contralateral extremity may be helpful for comparison in cases of suspected osteomyelitis. [19]

retrieved_docs.title: Sickle Cell Crisis -- History and Physical -- Osteonecrosis
retrieved_docs.content: Bone, as an organ, can undergo infarction in sickle cell disease, leading to osteonecrosis. Osteonecrosis affects upwards of 50% of sickle patients [16] . Plain X-rays, though helpful, aren't the gold standard, as is the MRI. The MRI is superior in sensitivity, specificity, and accuracy. [17] Osteonecrosis in sickle cell disease appears primarily in the long bones and secondarily in sites such as the cranial sections. Osteonecrosis can be found in other ailments, too, including steroid use, HIV, SLE, renal failure, multiple sclerosis, Sjogren syndrome, COVID, SARS (Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome), leukemias, and lymphomas. The skeletal vaso-occlusive disease leads to inflammation with cytokine release, increased receptor activation of nuclear factor Kappa-Beta Ligand (RANK) signaling, and osteoclast activation. [18] Osteonecrosis of the hip brings infirmity (as well as depression, anxiety, and a decreased quality of life - QOL) by causing subchondral bone collapse, leading to pain and disability. [19] Vitamin D deficiency also occurs in about a third of patients and compounds the situation. Protective factors for hip pathology were Hyrdrea use and high fetal hemoglobin. Some risk factors included an Hgb / Hct ratio more than 0.33 and the presence of BMP 6 (bone morphogenic protein) genotypes AT-rs267196 and AG-rs267201. [20] The latter is associated with chondroblasts and osteoblasts, valuable in bone formation. The treatment approach is multifaceted. Osteoblast (ABMDO) transplantation is being sought as a means to regenerate bone and promote healing. [21] Intravenous bisphosphonates have been found to induce bone resorption as well as improve or resolve pain. [18] As Pyrophonate analogs, osteoclasts take up bisphosphonates, thereby causing their apoptosis. Interactions can occur in any non-long bone location, such as the vertebrae, which gives the endplate an H-shaped deformity, and in the cranium. Cranial bone infarctions may involve the mandibular bone and cause facial nerve palsy; orbital infarcts may result in subgaleal hematomas. [18] [22] [23] The MRI can display bone marrow edema and subperiosteal fluid. Bone infarcts can initiate a secondary effect of myositis on nearby muscles. [18] Creatine phosphokinase (CPK) becomes elevated and can be used as a marker for this finding. Parallel sequelae (eg, acute fasciitis, necrotizing myositis, and compartmental syndrome) can be seen.

retrieved_docs.title: Sickle Cell Crisis -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell disease (SCD) is a group of inherited red blood cell disorders affecting about 1 in 500 African American children and 1 in 36,000 Hispanic American children. [1] SCD results in anemia and a "sickle cell crisis" (SCC). The main clinical feature of sickle cell disease is the "acute painful crisis," which often requires hospitalization. [2] The term "sickle cell crisis" is used to describe several acute conditions such as the vaso-occlusive crisis (acute painful crisis), aplastic crisis, splenic sequestration crisis, hyperhemolytic crisis, hepatic crisis, dactylitis, and acute chest syndrome. Other acute complications include pneumonia, meningitis, sepsis and osteomyelitis, stroke, avascular necrosis, priapism, and venous thromboembolism. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Transient Loss of Vision -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell disease

retrieved_docs.title: Osteomyelitis -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: The differential diagnosis for osteomyelitis is broad, and it is essential to keep these in mind during the evaluation of a patient with a suspected bone infection. These differentials include: Charcot arthropathy especially in people with diabetes SAPHO syndrome (synovitis, acne, pustulosis, hyperostosis, and osteitis) Arthritis including rheumatoid arthritis Metastatic bone disease Fracture, including pathological and stress fractures. Gout Avascular necrosis of the bone Bursitis Sickle cell vaso-occlusive pain crises

retrieved_docs.title: Legg-Calve-Perthes Disease -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Differential diagnoses that must be considered given the radiographic findings include: Infectious etiology including septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, pericapsular pyomyositis Transient synovitis Multiple epiphyseal dysplasia (MED) Spondyloepiphyseal dysplasia (SED) Sickle cell disease Gaucher disease Hypothyroidism Meyers dysplasia

retrieved_docs.title: Sickle Cell Anemia -- Evaluation -- Acute Ocular Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Orbital infarction: A rare complication that results from infarction of the surrounding orbital bone, which leads to an inflammatory response and compression of surrounding structures. Patients present with proptosis, local pain, and edema of the lid or orbit. The exam shows reduced extraocular motility and decreased visual acuity. CT scan helps in distinguishing this from orbital cellulitis/ infection.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2762
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell anemia is classified as داء الكريات المنجلية, and داء الكريات المنجلية is clinically associated with قساح.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1871
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell anemia is classified as داء الكريات المنجلية, and this disease is clinically associated with قساح. A principal manifestation of داء الكريات المنجلية is نقص الأكسجين في الدم (low blood oxygen), which is also mapped to الاِختِنَاق, indicating a link between hypoxemia and asphyxic events. Recognizing that patients with sickle cell anemia (داء الكريات المنجلية) may present with قساح and experience نقص الأكسجين في الدم that can progress to or be interpreted as الاِختِنَاق is important for accurate diagnosis, monitoring, and timely therapeutic intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1899
retrieved_docs.content: MISCELLANEOUS DIAGNOSTIC AND THERAPEUTIC PROCEDURES is a broad classification that encompasses a variety of diagnostic and therapeutic interventions, and within this category the specific procedure صورة نخاعية للمنطقة الشوكية is listed as a subordinate (child) procedure, indicating that spinal‑region imaging is considered one of the miscellaneous diagnostic and therapeutic techniques.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 2056
retrieved_docs.content: Computerized axial tomography (CT) scan and contrast arteriogram of femoral and lower extremity arteries are both categorized under the broader group labeled MISCELLANEOUS DIAGNOSTIC AND THERAPEUTIC PROCEDURES, indicating that they are considered part of a diverse set of imaging and interventional techniques used for diagnostic and therapeutic purposes. Within this same category, the procedure described in Arabic as صورة نخاعية للمنطقة الشوكية (spinal cord imaging of the spinal region) is identified as a child procedure, further illustrating that MISCELLANEOUS DIAGNOSTIC AND THERAPEUTIC PROCEDURES encompasses a range of specialized studies, from CT scanning and vascular contrast arteriography to spinal cord imaging, all of which contribute to comprehensive patient assessment and care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1702
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell anemia is classified as “داء الكريات المنجلية,” indicating that the two terms refer to the same hematologic disorder. In this context, the allowed qualifier “radioterapie” is applicable to “داء الكريات المنجلية,” meaning that radiotherapy is recognized as a permissible modifier or adjunct in the management or description of the disease. Additionally, the clinical feature or condition identified as “قساح” is clinically associated with “داء الكريات المنجلية,” suggesting that “قساح” commonly appears in the clinical presentation of sickle cell disease. The same term “قساح” can also qualify “radioterapie,” indicating that when radiotherapy is used, it may be described or modified by the characteristic “قساح.” Together, these relationships highlight that sickle cell anemia (داء الكريات المنجلية) is linked to the clinical entity “قساح,” and that radiotherapy is both an allowed qualifier for the disease and can itself be qualified by “قساح,” underscoring a nuanced interplay between the disease, its clinical manifestations, and therapeutic considerations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2848
retrieved_docs.content: حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ and ربط البوق are medical concepts that frequently co‑occur, indicating that the presence of حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ is often associated with the finding or procedure ربط البوق. This consistent association suggests that clinicians should be aware of the link between these two entities, as recognizing one may prompt evaluation for the other, thereby informing diagnosis and guiding appropriate patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 4 -- Community 2812
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell anemia, which is classified as داء الكريات المنجلية, is a hereditary hemoglobin disorder that can precipitate serious respiratory complications in adults. Clinically, داء الكريات المنجلية is associated with respiratory failure, respiratory insufficiency, and adult cardiac arrest, underscoring the need for vigilant respiratory monitoring and prompt intervention in patients affected by this disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1774
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell anemia is classified as داء الكريات المنجلية, the Arabic term for sickle cell disease. This condition is clinically associated with قَرحَة (ulcer), reflecting the propensity for vaso‑occlusive events and tissue ischemia that can lead to ulcer formation. A key manifestation of داء الكريات المنجلية is نقص الأكسجين في الدم (blood oxygen deficiency or hypoxemia), which results from impaired red‑cell flexibility and consequent reduced oxygen delivery. Recognizing that sickle cell anemia (داء الكريات المنجلية) can present with ulcers and low blood‑oxygen levels is important for comprehensive patient assessment and targeted management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 3 -- Community 2612
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell anemia, also referred to by its Arabic name داء الكريات المنجلية, is classified as a hemoglobinopathy. In the context of this disease, respiratory failure; insufficiency; arrest (adult) is identified as a clinical complication, and the Arabic term الاِختِنَاق (asphyxia) is likewise listed as an allowed qualifier complication. Both respiratory failure and asphyxia are closely linked to نقص الأكسجين في الدم (hypoxemia), which is a direct manifestation of داء الكريات المنجلية. The mapping of الاِختِنَاق to نقص الأكسجين في الدم highlights that low blood‑oxygen levels are a central pathophysiological feature that underlies these serious complications. Recognizing that sickle cell disease can precipitate severe respiratory compromise and hypoxemic states is essential for effective patient monitoring and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2441
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Abnormal” is used as an interpretation for both the Arabic phrase “البزل السّلوي غير الطبيعي” and the condition “فقر دم فانكوني.” In clinical practice, describing a spinal tap as “البزل السّلوي غير الطبيعي” indicates an abnormal cerebrospinal fluid result, while labeling “فقر دم فانكوني” as abnormal reflects the presence of Fanconi anemia, a hereditary bone‑marrow failure syndrome. Recognizing that “Abnormal” serves as a common descriptor for these distinct entities highlights its role as a generic marker of pathological or atypical findings in patient evaluation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 4 -- Community 2718
retrieved_docs.content: MISCELLANEOUS DIAGNOSTIC AND THERAPEUTIC PROCEDURES is classified under the broader category إجراء تدخلي, indicating that it is an interventional procedure. Within this group, the specific procedure صورة نخاعية للمنطقة الشوكية is considered a child or sub‑procedure, representing a spinal cord marrow imaging technique that falls under the miscellaneous diagnostic and therapeutic procedures umbrella.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2004
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Abnormal” is used as an interpretation of فقر دم فانكوني (Fanconi anemia), indicating that the clinical and laboratory findings associated with this genetic disorder are considered abnormal. Fanconi anemia is a hereditary condition characterized by bone‑marrow failure, congenital anomalies, and a heightened risk of malignancies, and the descriptor “Abnormal” reflects the pathological nature of its presentations.
